description: Fix insecure randomness (Math.random()) used in security-sensitive contexts. CodeQL flags Math.random() usage for generating IDs, tokens, and keys that should use cryptographically secure random values. Fix the main offenders: packages/common/src/utils.ts (generateId/generateUUID), apps/quantube/src/hooks/useLiveStream.ts (stream keys), apps/quantchat/src/hooks/useMedia.ts (media IDs), packages/agent-runtime/src/intelligent-agent.ts (plan/step IDs), and packages/moderation/src/services/video-moderator.ts (moderation result IDs).
steps: Fix packages/common/src/utils.ts - generateId() function (line 11-15): Replace Math.random().toString(36) with crypto.getRandomValues() or crypto.randomUUID(). Since this is a shared package used in both browser and Node, use globalThis.crypto.getRandomValues(new Uint8Array(N)) which is available in both environments (Node 19+ and all modern browsers). Convert to hex string.

Fix packages/common/src/utils.ts - generateUUID() function (line 21-35): Replace the manual Math.random()-based UUID generation with crypto.randomUUID() which is available in both Node 19+ and modern browsers. Add a fallback using crypto.getRandomValues() for older environments.

Fix packages/common/src/utils.ts - retry() function (line 167): The jitter in retry is not security-sensitive (just adds randomness to backoff timing), but to be thorough and avoid CodeQL flags, use crypto.getRandomValues() for the jitter calculation too.

Fix apps/quantube/src/hooks/useLiveStream.ts (lines 123, 238): Replace Math.random() for stream key generation with crypto.getRandomValues(). Stream keys are security-sensitive credentials. Keep Math.random() for viewer count simulation and quality fluctuation (those are UI-only non-security values).
Fix apps/quantchat/src/hooks/useMedia.ts (line 47): Replace the local generateId function that uses Math.random() with crypto.randomUUID() or crypto.getRandomValues().
Fix packages/agent-runtime/src/intelligent-agent.ts (line 62-63): Replace the local generateId function that uses Math.random() with crypto.randomUUID() based approach.

Fix packages/moderation/src/services/video-moderator.ts (line 120): Replace Math.random().toString(36) for moderation result ID with crypto.randomUUID() or crypto.getRandomValues(). Leave Math.random() for the simulation values (brightness, colorVariance, motionScore etc.) since those are mock/simulation data, not security-sensitive.
Verify: Run tests for affected packages.
acceptance_criteria: generateId() in packages/common/src/utils.ts uses cryptographically secure random
generateUUID() in packages/common/src/utils.ts uses crypto.randomUUID() with fallback
Stream key generation uses secure random
Agent runtime IDs use secure random
Moderation result IDs use secure random
Non-security Math.random() usage (UI simulations, jitter for backoff, mock data) is acceptable and left as-is OR replaced depending on CodeQL flags
verification: Run: cd /projects/sandbox/Quant-Ecosystem && pnpm --filter @quant/common test && pnpm --filter @quant/moderation test
findings: All 8 insecure randomness locations fixed with globalThis.crypto.getRandomValues() or globalThis.crypto.randomUUID(). Tests pass: common (39/39), recommendations (114/114), moderation (332/332 actual tests, 2 suites fail from pre-existing missing zod dep), agent-runtime (87/87 actual tests, 32 suites fail from pre-existing missing zod dep). Non-security Math.random() usage (viewer simulation, bitrate jitter, fps, brightness, motionScore, etc.) left untouched as intended. Prettier reformatted via lint-staged.
